title: The Sealed Book
subtitle: Ludolph closes his Life of Christ and seals it in the Name of Jesus

stillness: Turn and turn again, says Bernard,
and you will find no peace, no rest,
Be still here, in this Name.
gospel: My Beloved is mine, and I am his.
Between my breasts he shall abide,
that is, among my affections he shall dwell.
Let your tears be your bread by day and by night,
until you appear in the sight of the Lord Jesus,
and are received into his embrace.
behold: Call to mind now, in the secret of your heart,
the Lord Jesus, conceived of the Virgin for us without stain,
born without harm to her virginity,
wrapped in swaddling-bands, laid in a manger,
washing the feet of his disciples and wiping them with a towel,
pouring out a sweat of blood,
sold for thirty pieces of silver, betrayed with a kiss,
seized with swords and clubs, bound, judged,
led like an innocent lamb to the slaughter,
not opening his mouth when he was ill-treated,
given vinegar to drink, fed with gall,
pouring out all his blood through the five wounds of his body,
commending his beloved soul into the hands of the Father,
bowing his head, and giving up the spirit:
and all of this he bore for us.
consider: You have here the Life of our Lord Jesus Christ,
given to you, in great part, for your meditations.
It is a seedbed of spiritual meditations,
that from it the fruit of divine love may rise in you and grow:
that meditation may stir up affection,
affection give birth to desire,
and desire wring out tears.
For these are the things the Lord Jesus did in the flesh;
yet far higher is it to gaze upon him in the spirit,
and to this you may climb by this ladder.
Who would not rejoice and exult, seeing his Maker
not only become man for him,
but endure things so hard and so unworthy?
What is more sweetly chewed over in the mind?
What is more sweetly tasted? What more gladly conceived?
Jesus is the gladness of mourners,
the eternity of the living, the sweetness of the grieving,
the abundance of the needy, the health of the languishing.
If you would rest, set Jesus as a seal upon your heart,
for he, being tranquil himself, makes all things tranquil.
Love him, then, who so greatly loved you.
This book, sealed in the Name of the Lord Jesus,
who is the sealed book, is here closed and sealed.
prayer: O Jesus Christ, Son of the living God,
for these things, and for all that we devoutly believe of you
and ought to believe,
we render you thanks, though unworthy;
and through them we humbly beseech you,
that you offer your very self to God the Father
on behalf of all things in heaven, on earth, and beneath the earth:
for the worthy praise of all,
in full thanksgiving for every gift owed by each creature,
and for the true amendment of all the evils
committed from the beginning of the world,
and for the satisfaction of all the good ever left undone.
O my sweetest Jesus, hope of the sighing soul,
the tears of the devout seek you,
the cry of the inmost mind calls out to you.
Wherever I shall be, my Jesus I desire.
How glad, when I have found him! How happy, when I hold him!
Now what I sought, I see; what I longed for, I hold;
I languish for the love of Jesus, and with my whole heart I burn.
Amen.

latinJewel.latin: Gira et regira, versa et reversa, et non invenies pacem et requiem, nisi in solo Jesu.
latinJewel.english: Turn and turn again, search and search once more, and you will find no peace and no rest, except in Jesus alone.
